summary: Agent Role Builder role resume_required: Budget exhausted after 3 rounds. 4 unresolved.

participant_records.verdict: {"status":"pushback","rationale":"Pushback is required because reviewer-codex-r0 issued a reject with blocking and major findings, and the self-check independently confirms the blocking canonical-output failure. Under the approved review-process architecture, blocking/major findings are material, freeze is not allowed while they remain unresolved, and arbitration cannot be used on them.","unresolved":["Canonical output paths are not defined as one authoritative contract: <outputs> and Step 5 use slug/basename forms while the required canonical files live under tools/agent-role-builder/role/, and self-check already flags all four required outputs as missing.","Artifact lifecycle and terminal-state behavior are not mechanically derivable: the canonical directory is unnamed, decision-log and board-summary preserve/append/replace behavior is undefined, and resume_required has no explicit trigger distinct from pushback or blocked.","Governance authority order conflicts with approved architecture: the role says it is subordinate to static docs instead of treating live COO/memory-engine governance as primary and docs as reference evidence.","Freeze and arbitration semantics conflict with the approved review process: the draft requires every reviewer to approve, omits the conditional/frozen_with_conditions path for minor-only outcomes, and introduces 'arbitrate after 2+ consecutive splits' even though arbitration is minor-only and cannot override material pushback.","The contract intent is run-specific/bootstrap-specific rather than a stable standing mission for the role package."],"improvements_applied":[],"arbitration_used":false,"arbitration_rationale":null}

{"verdict":"reject","conceptual_groups":[{"id":"group-1","summary":"Authority is anchored to the wrong sources and the write boundary is underspecified.","severity":"major","findings":[{"id":"f1","description":"The authority chain says the role is subordinate to `docs/v0/architecture.md` and `docs/VISION.md`, but the bootstrap and architecture sources say the COO controller governs turns and runtime rules come from the memory engine. Treating static docs as operative authority is an authority-model mismatch.","source_section":"<authority>"},{"id":"f2","description":"`Filesystem writes outside role package directories` is too narrow for the described workflow, which also produces run-scoped drafts, rounds, runtime state, decision logs, and summaries outside the canonical role directory. The write boundary is therefore unclear and inaccurate.","source_section":"<authority>"}],"redesign_guidance":"Define authority in terms of the COO-governed runtime contract first, with static docs as reference evidence only. Then state the write boundary precisely: canonical role package paths plus the current run directory and any explicitly governed support artifacts."},{"id":"group-2","summary":"Several board-governance rules are invented or stricter than the schema/runtime contract.","severity":"major","findings":[{"id":"f3","description":"The role text requires one live Codex leader, but the request schema allows the leader provider to be `codex`, `claude`, or `gemini`; only reviewer pairs are constrained to Codex+Claude. The leader restriction is therefore not grounded in the schema shown.","source_section":"<role>"},{"id":"f4","description":"The freeze rule says every reviewer must approve before freeze, but the actual board contract/runtime uses `approved` or `conditional` as non-blocking and freezes when no blocking or major issues remain. This markdown rule overconstrains the process and conflicts with the contract semantics.","source_section":"<guardrails> / <steps>"}],"redesign_guidance":"Only state board rules that are actually enforced by the request schema and runtime. Separate reviewer-pair requirements from leader-provider rules, and express freeze criteria in the same materiality terms used by the review contract."},{"id":"group-3","summary":"Artifact lifecycle is incomplete and internally inconsistent across steps, outputs, and completion.","severity":"major","findings":[{"id":"f5","description":"The `<outputs>` section lists generic canonical filenames but does not reference the canonical `required_outputs` paths from the contract summary, which is why the self-check is correctly flagging missing required outputs.","source_section":"<outputs>"},{"id":"f6","description":"The workflow says `result.json`, decision log, and board summary are written during resolution, but the outputs section omits the run-scoped role markdown, role contract, decision log, and board summary artifacts that exist before freeze and remain evidence-only on non-frozen runs.","source_section":"<steps> / <outputs>"},{"id":"f7","description":"Completion criteria mix output-dir completion with `Decision log updated (on freeze)`, which obscures that non-frozen terminal statuses still complete and still need explicit evidence artifacts, while canonical promotion happens only on freeze.","source_section":"<completion>"}],"redesign_guidance":"Model lifecycle in three explicit tiers: canonical promotion targets from `required_outputs`, run-scoped evidence artifacts in `output_dir`, and internal audit artifacts. Then align completion rules with all terminal statuses, not just freeze behavior."}],"residual_risks":["Reviewers may reject valid runs because the markdown freeze rule is stricter than the implemented `approved/conditional` semantics.","Future updates may drift further if static docs remain framed as operative authority instead of runtime-governed evidence.","Artifact promotion and non-frozen evidence handling will stay ambiguous until canonical versus run-scoped outputs are explicitly separated."],"strengths":["All required top-level tags are present, so the package has a usable structural skeleton.","The core mission is clear: governed role-package creation with pushback instead of invented semantics.","The draft already emphasizes provenance, reviewer pairing, and non-freeze when material pushback remains, which is the right overall direction."]}

{"status":"pushback","rationale":"Pushback. Both reviewers rejected with material findings: reviewer-claude reported blocking output-path/evidence-chain failures, and reviewer-codex reported major authority, governance, and lifecycle defects. These are fixable draft/governance issues rather than a non-recoverable blocker, so blocked is not appropriate.","unresolved":["Canonical artifact paths are not fully rooted/aligned in <outputs> and contract package_files with tools/agent-role-builder/role/...; self-check still reports the required canonical outputs as missing.","Authority precedence is incorrect: the draft treats static docs as operative authority instead of COO/runtime governance, and the write boundary does not accurately cover run-scoped drafts and evidence artifacts.","Board-governance semantics are invented or overconstrained: the draft requires a Codex leader and unanimous approve semantics instead of the runtime's no-material-pushback freeze rule using approved/conditional outcomes.","Artifact lifecycle is still inconsistent across <steps>, <outputs>, and <completion>; canonical promotion, run-scoped evidence, and per-terminal-state completion are not defined as one authoritative matrix.","<scope> still contains duplicate or contradictory out-of-scope statements, especially around application workflow and runtime orchestration.","Round 0 material findings are not explicitly mapped forward or resolved; there is no evidence chain showing the prior 5 unresolved issues were addressed."],"improvements_applied":["All required top-level XML tags are present, and Step 3 explicitly enumerates the required tag set.","The draft separates outputs into canonical, evidence, and internal artifact groups instead of one undifferentiated list.","The board-review flow includes split-verdict handling plus provenance and reviewer-pair guardrails."],"arbitration_used":false,"arbitration_rationale":null}

{"status":"pushback","rationale":"Pushback is required because both reviewers returned reject and material findings remain. The draft still has a blocking canonical-artifact/output-lifecycle defect confirmed by the self-check, plus major authority and governance defects. These are fixable role-definition issues, not an unrecoverable execution failure, so blocked is not appropriate.","unresolved":["Canonical artifact authority is still unresolved: <outputs>, Step 5, and contract package_files do not declare the full canonical tools/agent-role-builder/role/... promotion targets, the four MISSING_REQUIRED_OUTPUT self-check failures remain, and canonical vs run-scoped artifact lifecycle is still not defined as one authoritative matrix.","Authority precedence is still unclear: the role treats static docs as operative subordination, does not define what wins across COO/runtime governance, request, contract, and reference docs, and mixes request-time/runtime knobs into durable role semantics without a stated authority basis.","Board-governance semantics still include unsupported or incomplete rules: the Codex-only leader requirement is not source-grounded, Step 4 arbitration diverges from the approved minor-only arbitration model, and arbitration mechanics/limits are not coherently defined across markdown and contract.","Durable package semantics are still unstable: the contract intent leaks bootstrap/run-specific context into canonical identity, and terminal-state completion obligations for non-frozen outcomes are not fully specified."],"improvements_applied":["All required top-level XML-tagged sections are present, and Step 3 explicitly enumerates the required tag set.","The draft now separates outputs into canonical, evidence, and internal artifact groups instead of one undifferentiated list.","The review flow now explicitly includes split-verdict handling plus provenance, reviewer-pair, and decision-history guardrails."],"arbitration_used":false,"arbitration_rationale":null}

validation_issues.message: Required output "tools/agent-role-builder/role/agent-role-builder-role.md" not referenced in <outputs> section
Required output "tools/agent-role-builder/role/agent-role-builder-role-contract.json" not referenced in <outputs> section
Required output "tools/agent-role-builder/role/agent-role-builder-decision-log.md" not referenced in <outputs> section
Required output "tools/agent-role-builder/role/agent-role-builder-board-summary.md" not referenced in <outputs> section

open_questions: Canonical output paths are not defined as one authoritative contract: <outputs> and Step 5 use slug/basename forms while the required canonical files live under tools/agent-role-builder/role/, and self-check already flags all four required outputs as missing.

Artifact lifecycle and terminal-state behavior are not mechanically derivable: the canonical directory is unnamed, decision-log and board-summary preserve/append/replace behavior is undefined, and resume_required has no explicit trigger distinct from pushback or blocked.

Governance authority order conflicts with approved architecture: the role says it is subordinate to static docs instead of treating live COO/memory-engine governance as primary and docs as reference evidence.

Freeze and arbitration semantics conflict with the approved review process: the draft requires every reviewer to approve, omits the conditional/frozen_with_conditions path for minor-only outcomes, and introduces 'arbitrate after 2+ consecutive splits' even though arbitration is minor-only and cannot override material pushback.
The contract intent is run-specific/bootstrap-specific rather than a stable standing mission for the role package.

Canonical artifact paths are not fully rooted/aligned in <outputs> and contract package_files with tools/agent-role-builder/role/...; self-check still reports the required canonical outputs as missing.

Authority precedence is incorrect: the draft treats static docs as operative authority instead of COO/runtime governance, and the write boundary does not accurately cover run-scoped drafts and evidence artifacts.

Board-governance semantics are invented or overconstrained: the draft requires a Codex leader and unanimous approve semantics instead of the runtime's no-material-pushback freeze rule using approved/conditional outcomes.

Artifact lifecycle is still inconsistent across <steps>, <outputs>, and <completion>; canonical promotion, run-scoped evidence, and per-terminal-state completion are not defined as one authoritative matrix.
<scope> still contains duplicate or contradictory out-of-scope statements, especially around application workflow and runtime orchestration.
Round 0 material findings are not explicitly mapped forward or resolved; there is no evidence chain showing the prior 5 unresolved issues were addressed.

Canonical artifact authority is still unresolved: <outputs>, Step 5, and contract package_files do not declare the full canonical tools/agent-role-builder/role/... promotion targets, the four MISSING_REQUIRED_OUTPUT self-check failures remain, and canonical vs run-scoped artifact lifecycle is still not defined as one authoritative matrix.

Authority precedence is still unclear: the role treats static docs as operative subordination, does not define what wins across COO/runtime governance, request, contract, and reference docs, and mixes request-time/runtime knobs into durable role semantics without a stated authority basis.

Board-governance semantics still include unsupported or incomplete rules: the Codex-only leader requirement is not source-grounded, Step 4 arbitration diverges from the approved minor-only arbitration model, and arbitration mechanics/limits are not coherently defined across markdown and contract.

Durable package semantics are still unstable: the contract intent leaks bootstrap/run-specific context into canonical identity, and terminal-state completion obligations for non-frozen outcomes are not fully specified.
